title: Individual Agentic Engineering Maturity Rubric
description: Drives the workspace self-assessment + learning-journey skill. Places a person on the AEM Spine (L1-L5) and scores five cross-cutting Axes (0-4). The Spine level is CAPPED at L3 when the Verification axis scores <= 1 (the core AEM rule). Output maps the weakest axis to prescribed patterns via patternMap.

spine.name: Prompting
spine.placement: I type requests into a chat and use what comes back; I rarely read diffs.
spine.distinguishingSkill: Knowing how to ask in natural language.

spine.name: Prompt Engineering
spine.placement: I keep reusable prompts (roles, examples, format constraints) and review diffs before accepting.
spine.distinguishingSkill: Repeatable prompt design for discrete, one-shot tasks.

spine.name: Context Engineering
spine.tagline: Build the system that feeds the model
spine.placement: I maintain a personal/project CLAUDE.md, curate my tools/MCPs, and manage context deliberately (/clear, /compact, just-in-time retrieval).

spine.name: Agentic Engineering
spine.tagline: Delegate, verify, stay in the loop
spine.placement: I run agents in a loop against a written spec + a check they can run, and I review every diff. A meaningful share of my work is delegated-and-verified.
spine.distinguishingSkill: Running agents against specs + verifiable success criteria, reviewing every diff.

spine.name: Agentic Orchestration
spine.placement: I orchestrate multiple/parallel agents, run my own evals, and feed learnings back across sessions.
spine.distinguishingSkill: Task decomposition, managing context across agents, building systems that improve without re-engineering.

axes.title: Verification
axes.note: The differentiator. Caps the Spine at L3 if at the bottom.

bands.label: Agent runs its own check (tests/screenshot/goal); personal eval harness
Curated CLAUDE.md + subagents isolate exploration + compaction
Approves every action, or blindly auto-accepts
Dials leash per risk; earns longer leash via proven checks; sandboxes the rest
Corrections + memory feed back systematically; builds skills/commands

questions.prompt: Pick the highest statement that is reliably true under pressure (not your best day).
When an agent finishes a task, how do you confirm it's correct?
How do you manage what the model sees across a working session?
How do you decide how much an agent does on its own?
When an agent makes a mistake, what happens next time?
How aware are you of token cost and tier/permission discipline?
Roughly what % of your work is delegated-and-verified to agents (the 'and-verified' matters)?

patternTitles.A3: Point at examples, name the files
patternTitles.B2: Give it a check it can run
patternTitles.B3: Spec-first: let the agent interview you
patternTitles.B5: Watch it like a hawk (review every diff)
patternTitles.C1: If you do it twice a day, make it a command/skill
